eGalvanic Deep Bug Verification Report

Interactive Playwright testing across 15+ pages — verified through form submissions, CRUD operations, navigation testing, and console analysis
📅 April 20, 2026 🌐 acme.qa.egalvanic.ai (V1.21) 👤 abhiyant.singh+admin@egalvanic.com 🖥️ Chromium 136 / 1920×1080 ⏱️ 5 test sessions + PDF cross-verification, ~60 minutes total

PDF Bugs Cross-Checked

Testing Methodology

  • Approach: Deep interactive testing using Playwright browser automation (non-headless Chromium). Each page was visited, forms were opened and submitted, buttons were clicked, validation was tested, and console/network errors were captured.
  • What counts as a bug: Broken functionality, incorrect behavior, missing error handling, silent API failures, UX inconsistencies. NOT included: empty data (environment state), test data pollution, "0 rows" displays.
  • 5 test batches: Rounds 1 & 2 — CRUD, admin, audit log, SLDs, Equipment Library, Issues validation, keyboard a11y. Round 3 (PDF cross-verification) — ran all 32 bugs from Combined Bug Report (March 5, 2026) against live platform to determine which remain, which are fixed, and which were environment-state artifacts.
  • Sentry integration observed: The app sends 20+ events per testing session to Sentry (o4509671240105984.ingest.us.sentry.io). Sentry is actively capturing frontend errors — errors flagged in this report are likely also visible in Sentry's issue dashboard.

Pages Tested:

Site Overview (Dashboard)
Tasks
Work Orders
Opportunities
Assets
Connections
Locations
Issues

Equipment Library
Arc Flash Readiness

Admin Settings (5 tabs)

Work Order Planning
BUG-001
No 404 error page — invalid URLs render blank content area
MEDIUM

Page

Any invalid URL (e.g., /nonexistent-page-xyz-404)

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as admin user
  2. Navigate to any invalid URL, e.g., /nonexistent-page-xyz-404
  3. Observe the page content area

Expected Behavior

A proper 404 "Page Not Found" page should display with a helpful message, a link back to the dashboard, and possibly a search or navigation aid.

Actual Behavior

The page shows the sidebar navigation but the content area is completely blank. No 404 message, no "Page Not Found" text, no redirect. The user has no indication they've reached an invalid URL.

Impact

Users who mistype URLs or follow broken links have no feedback. They see a blank screen and may think the app is broken. This also affects SEO and user trust.

Suggested Fix

Add a catch-all route in React Router that renders a 404 component. The component should show "Page Not Found" message with a "Go to Dashboard" button.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080

Screenshot Evidence

p2-404-page.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-002
Audit Log has duplicate search fields — confusing UX
MEDIUM

Page

/admin/audit-log

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as admin user
  2. Navigate to Settings (sidebar) > Audit Log
  3. Observe the search/filter area at the top of the page

Expected Behavior

A single unified search field for filtering audit log entries.

Actual Behavior

Two separate search fields are visible: (1) "Search..." input in the top filter bar next to Status, Entity Type, and Time Range dropdowns, and (2) "Search items..." input in the MUI DataGrid toolbar area on the right side. Both are fully functional but search different things, creating user confusion about which to use.

Impact

Users may not know which search field to use. One searches the API, the other filters client-side DataGrid rows. This leads to inconsistent results and confusion.

Suggested Fix

Remove one of the search fields. Either: (a) hide the DataGrid's built-in search toolbar using `disableColumnFilter` or custom toolbar, or (b) remove the custom search field and rely solely on DataGrid's built-in search.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, Time Range: Last Hour

Screenshot Evidence

p2-audit-log.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-003
DevRev PLuG SDK fails to initialize on every page — support widget broken
MEDIUM

Page

All pages

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as admin user
  2. Open Browser DevTools (F12) > Console tab
  3. Navigate to any page (Dashboard, Tasks, Assets, etc.)
  4. Observe console errors

Expected Behavior

PLuG SDK should initialize successfully, providing the DevRev customer support chat widget.

Actual Behavior

"[PLuG] Error initializing with authentication: {}" appears in the console on EVERY page navigation. Occurred 17 times across 12 pages during testing. The empty object {} suggests the authentication token/config is not being passed to PLuG correctly. The support widget is non-functional.

Impact

The DevRev customer support chat widget does not work. Users cannot access in-app support. Additionally, the repeated failed initialization adds unnecessary network requests and console noise on every page load.

Suggested Fix

Check the PLuG SDK initialization code. The empty auth object {} indicates the DevRev authentication token is not being set. Verify: (1) DevRev API key is configured, (2) User token is being passed during PLuG.init(), (3) The initialization timing is correct (after auth is available).

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, 17 occurrences across 12 pages

Screenshot Evidence

p3-dashboard-full.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-004
Content Security Policy blocks Beamer notification fonts — 102+ CSP violations per session
MEDIUM

Page

All pages

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as admin user
  2. Open Browser DevTools (F12) > Console tab
  3. Navigate to any page
  4. Observe CSP violation errors

Expected Behavior

The Content Security Policy should allow Beamer notification widget fonts to load without errors.

Actual Behavior

102 CSP violations logged during a single test session across 12 pages. The font-src directive is set to "'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data:" which does NOT include "https://app.getbeamer.com". Six different Beamer Lato font files are blocked: Lato-Normal-300a/300b/400a/400b/700a/700b.woff2. Each page load generates 6+ CSP violations.

Impact

Beamer notification widget renders with fallback fonts instead of its intended Lato font family. This causes visual inconsistency in the notification panel. The 102+ console errors per session create noise that obscures real errors during debugging.

Suggested Fix

Update the Content-Security-Policy header to add "https://app.getbeamer.com" to the font-src directive. Change: font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com data: → font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com https://app.getbeamer.com data:

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, 102 violations across 12 pages

Screenshot Evidence

p3-dashboard-full.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-005
Task creation triggers API 400 on task-session mapping — backend association fails silently
MEDIUM

Page

/tasks

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Navigate to /tasks
  2. Click "+ Add Tasks"
  3. Fill in Title field (e.g., "Test Task")
  4. Leave other fields at defaults (Task Type: PM, no asset, no procedure)
  5. Click "Create Task"
  6. Open DevTools Console and observe errors

Expected Behavior

Task should be created successfully with all associated mappings. No console errors.

Actual Behavior

The task IS created and appears in the list. However, the API call to /api/mapping/task-session/create returns HTTP 400. Console shows: "[useEntityAssociation] Failed to create task-session mapping". The task-session association silently fails, potentially breaking downstream features that depend on this mapping (session tracking, workflow linking, audit trail).

Impact

Tasks are created without proper session mappings. This may cause issues with task-session associations, potentially affecting work order linking, reporting, and task lifecycle management. The silent failure means the bug is invisible to end users but may cause data integrity issues.

Suggested Fix

Investigate the /api/mapping/task-session/create endpoint. The 400 response suggests invalid payload or missing required fields. Check if session ID is being generated/passed correctly. The useEntityAssociation hook needs error handling that either retries or shows user feedback.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, Site: Yxbzjzxj

Screenshot Evidence

p3-task-after-create.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-006
Issue created without required Issue Class — frontend + backend validation gap
MEDIUM

Page

/issues

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Navigate to /issues
  2. Click "+ Create Issue"
  3. Fill Title: "Test Issue Title"
  4. Fill Proposed Resolution: "Test proposed resolution"
  5. Leave Issue Class empty (shows "Select an issue class") — note it is marked required with asterisk *
  6. Click "Create Issue"

Expected Behavior

Validation error should appear for Issue Class (marked required with *). The form should NOT submit without Issue Class.

Actual Behavior

The issue is created successfully — API POST /api/issue/create returns HTTP 201. The form closes, and the issue appears in the list with Issue Class column showing "—" (empty). No validation error is ever shown for the Issue Class field. Both frontend validation AND backend API validation are missing for this required field.

Impact

Issues can be created without classification. This breaks: (1) Filtering/reporting by issue class, (2) NEC violation tracking, (3) Data integrity — existing issues all have "NEC Violation" class, but new ones can be created without any class. (4) The * asterisk marking on Issue Class misleads users into thinking it is enforced.

Suggested Fix

Two fixes needed: (1) Frontend: Add client-side validation for Issue Class dropdown — show "Issue class is required" error when empty on submit. (2) Backend: API should return 400 with validation error when issue_class is null/empty in the POST body.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, Site: Yxbzjzxj, verified via POST /api/issue/create → 201

Screenshot Evidence

r2p3-issue-submit-no-class.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-007
No visible keyboard focus indicator during Tab navigation — accessibility issue
LOW

Page

All pages

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Navigate to any page (e.g., /dashboard)
  2. Press the Tab key repeatedly (10+ times) to move focus through interactive elements

Expected Behavior

Each focused element should have a visible focus ring or outline (per WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible — Level AA requirement).

Actual Behavior

Focus moves through elements (confirmed via document.activeElement inspection: Tab landed on an element with text "Equipment Library") but outline-style is "none" and outline-width is "0px". Users navigating via keyboard cannot see which element is currently focused.

Impact

Keyboard-only users and users with motor impairments cannot navigate the application effectively. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level AA failure (Success Criterion 2.4.7 Focus Visible). May affect ADA/Section 508 compliance.

Suggested Fix

Add a global CSS rule: *:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #3b82f6; outline-offset: 2px; } — or use MUI's built-in focus ripple system. Ensure all interactive elements (links, buttons, inputs) have visible focus indicators.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080

Screenshot Evidence

p3-dashboard-full.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-008
Raw HTTP 400 error + internal API details exposed in UI for invalid asset URLs
HIGH

Page

/assets/:invalidId

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as any user
  2. Navigate to an invalid asset URL, e.g., https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/assets/invalid-uuid-test-12345
  3. Wait 4-5 seconds for the page to load

Expected Behavior

A friendly 404 / "Asset not found" page, OR redirect to /assets. Raw backend error messages and HTTP status codes must not be exposed to end users.

Actual Behavior

Page shows raw error banner: "Failed to fetch enriched node details: 400" — leaks both the internal endpoint name ("enriched node details") and the HTTP status code directly to the user. No recovery action offered.

Impact

Security: reveals backend API naming and endpoint structure (recon data for attackers). UX: user is stranded with a technical error and no way forward. This was originally reported March 5, 2026 and is still present on April 20 — over 45 days unfixed.

Suggested Fix

Add a global API error boundary in React that catches 4xx/5xx responses and renders a user-friendly error page. Never show raw backend error messages in the UI. For asset-specific 404s, redirect to /assets with a toast notification.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, verified 2026-04-20 against live platform

Screenshot Evidence

pdf-bug005-raw-400-error.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-009
JSON parse error exposed to user for invalid session/work order URLs
HIGH

Page

/sessions/:invalidId

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Log in as any user
  2. Navigate to an invalid work order URL, e.g., https://acme.qa.egalvanic.ai/sessions/invalid-session-id-9999
  3. Wait 4-5 seconds

Expected Behavior

A friendly error page or redirect to /sessions. API should return JSON 404 and frontend should handle it gracefully.

Actual Behavior

Raw JavaScript error visible: "Unexpected token '<', '

Impact

Exposes implementation details (tells users/attackers the backend serves HTML error pages for unknown IDs). Frontend has no global error handler for JSON parse failures — any API returning non-JSON crashes the page. This has been broken for 45+ days.

Suggested Fix

Two fixes: (1) Backend: ensure API endpoints return Content-Type: application/json with structured JSON error bodies for 4xx/5xx. (2) Frontend: wrap JSON.parse in try/catch inside the fetch layer, render a generic error UI on failure. Add a global API interceptor.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, verified 2026-04-20

Screenshot Evidence

pdf-bug006-json-parse-error.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-010
Invalid Service Agreement URLs show completely blank page
MEDIUM

Page

/jobs-v2/:invalidId

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Navigate to an invalid service agreement URL like /jobs-v2/invalid-uuid-12345
  2. Observe the content area

Expected Behavior

Either a 404 "Not Found" page or a redirect back to /jobs-v2.

Actual Behavior

Completely blank white page — no error banner, no redirect, no back button. User sees only the sidebar with no indication of what happened. This is separate from BUG-001 (generic 404): the /jobs-v2/:id route is matched but its loader silently fails.

Impact

Users stranded with no recovery path. This is a specific failure mode of the /jobs-v2 route — likely a missing error boundary inside the Service Agreement detail component.

Suggested Fix

Add an error boundary to the /jobs-v2/:id route component that renders a "Service Agreement not found" UI when the loader/query returns a 404. Also add a "Back to Service Agreements" link.

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, verified 2026-04-20

Screenshot Evidence

pdf-bug011-blank-job.png — captured 2026-04-20
BUG-011
Form validation uses internal field names instead of user-facing labels
MEDIUM

Page

/assets (Create Asset form)

Steps to Reproduce

  1. Click "+ Create Asset"
  2. Leave all fields empty
  3. Click "Create Asset" submit button
  4. Observe the validation error text below each field

Expected Behavior

Errors should use the user-facing field labels, e.g., "Asset Name is required", "Asset Class is required".

Actual Behavior

Errors read: "Asset label is required" and "Asset type is required". These are internal field keys (label, type) from the form schema, not the labels the user actually sees on screen ("Name", "Class"). This breaks mental mapping between the visible label and the error message.

Impact

Confuses users ("What is asset label? I didn't see a label field"). Exposes internal field naming convention. Suggests validation is not going through the same i18n/labels pipeline as the form render.

Suggested Fix

Centralize field label strings (e.g., in a fieldLabels.ts map) and reference them from both the form render and the validation schema (Yup/Zod). Example: yup.string().required(`${fieldLabels.assetName} is required`).

Environment

V1.21, Chromium 136, 1920x1080, verified 2026-04-20

Screenshot Evidence

pdf-bug020-internal-labels.png — captured 2026-04-20

Console & API Error Summary

Captured across 12 pages during Part 3 testing session (140 total console errors, 20 API errors)

Category Count Unique Errors Impact
CSP Violations (Beamer fonts) 102 6 unique font files blocked See BUG-003 — Beamer widget renders with fallback fonts
PLuG SDK (DevRev support widget) 17 1 unique error See BUG-003 — Support widget non-functional
Resource Load Failures (HTTP 429) 16 1 unique (Beamer tracking rate-limited) Beamer analytics being rate-limited. Low impact.
HTTP Errors (401/400) 4 2 unique (auth/me 401, auth/refresh 400) Initial auth negotiation. Expected during login flow.
Application Error 1 useEntityAssociation mapping failure See BUG-005 — Task-session mapping fails

API Error Breakdown

EndpointStatusCountNotes

/api/auth/me4011Expected — pre-login check
/api/auth/refresh4001Expected — token refresh during login
backend.getbeamer.com/track4001Beamer tracking failure

Pages Verified as Working Correctly

The following pages were interactively tested and found to be functioning as expected:

  • Site Overview (Dashboard) — All 6 stat cards load correctly (Assets 21, Issues 9, Tasks 7, WOs 16, Opps $24.2k, Equipment at Risk $295.0k). Pie charts render. Site selector works.
  • Tasks — CRUD works: list displays, Add Task panel opens with proper fields (Task Type, Asset, Procedure, Title, Description, Due Date, Photos), form validation catches required fields, task creation succeeds.
  • Assets — 21 assets listed, click-through to detail works, detail shows Core Attributes/OCP/Connections/Issues/Tasks/Photos/Attachments tabs. Search input functional.
  • Arc Flash Readiness — Overview shows progress gauges (70% Asset Details, 8% Connections, 29% Connection Details, 0% Eng Approved). Per-type breakdown: Busway 100%, Circuit Breaker 97%, Fuse 0%, Generator 100%. Tabs work.
  • Scheduling — Calendar renders with Quarter/Month/Week/Day views, all view toggles work, Today/Back/Next navigation works. Shows WO items.
  • Admin Settings — Sites tab loads with correct columns and "Create Site" button. Users/Classes/PM/Forms tabs present.
  • Connections — Lists 2 rows, "Add" button present.
  • Opportunities — Create form opens correctly with auto-filled Facility and required Opportunity Name field.
  • Panel Schedules, Commitments, Equipment Library, Condition Assessment, Work Order Planning — All render content and navigation works.

Generated by Playwright automated deep testing • Report by abhiyant.singh@egalvanic.com • April 20, 2026

5 test sessions + 32 PDF bugs cross-verified × 22 pages = 11 verified bugs (2 HIGH, 8 MEDIUM, 1 LOW) | Quality over quantity
